probes.evidence: psql -c 'select 1' over a fresh connection returned: 1
psql \bind 42 + 'where trip_id = $1' (extended query protocol) returned: 42|Brussels

\dt demo.* lists trips and cities (plus $snapshots/$manifests metadata tables): demo|cities|table|postgres demo|cities$manifests|table|postgres demo|cities$snapshots|table|postgres demo|trips|table|postgres demo|trips$manifests|table|postgres demo|trips$snapshots|table|postgres
information_schema.tables for schema demo returned: cities cities$manifests cities$snapshots trips trips$manifests trips$snapshots
8 parallel psql SELECT count(*) connections all succeeded (8/8 identical results: 281)
no auth enforcement: connections with a wrong password and with nonexistent user 'not_a_real_user' both succeeded (select 1 -> 1). AuthManager::default() is a noop.
no TLS support: psql: error: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5439 failed: server does not support SSL, but SSL was required
INSERT trip_id=900001 returned tag 'INSERT 0 1'; row read back over a NEW connection: 900001|Parity B1
UPDATE rejected (iceberg-datafusion 0.9 is append-only): ERROR: UPDATE operation on table 'demo.trips' caused by This feature is not implemented: UPDATE not supported for Base table
DELETE rejected (iceberg-datafusion 0.9 is append-only): ERROR: DELETE operation on table 'demo.trips' caused by This feature is not implemented: DELETE not supported for Base table
BEGIN/ROLLBACK are accepted on the wire but are non-transactional: INSERT inside BEGIN..ROLLBACK persisted (row 900002 visible after ROLLBACK, count=1). Session output: BEGIN INSERT 0 1 ROLLBACK
no constraint enforcement: duplicate insert of trip_id=900002 accepted ('INSERT 0 1'), table now holds 2 rows with that id. Iceberg has no PK/unique constraints.

probes.behavior: data in open format, other engines can read
probes.evidence: independent reader pyiceberg 0.11.1 read demo.trips via the REST catalog + S3: 284 rows == server's count(284)
Lakekeeper GET /v1/563d3f92-7884-11f1-ab2a-cba89bbcc662/namespaces/demo/tables lists: ["cities","trips"]

Moonlink exists to replicate Postgres heap data into Iceberg; icegres data is born in Iceberg (INSERT via wire commits an Iceberg snapshot directly, verified by B1+C1) — there is no second copy to synchronize.
row committed via conn A was readable from a new connection ~46ms after commit (coarse; includes psql startup — precise p50/p95 is bench freshness_ms). Moonlink bar: sub-second.

demo."trips$snapshots" queryable (5 snapshots listed; $manifests also works). Caveats: 'select *' fails on the Map-typed summary column (ERROR: Unsupported Datatype Map("key_value": non-null Struct("key": non-null Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "7"}, "value": Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "8"}), unsorted)) and count(*) hits a DataFusion logical/physical schema mismatch — column projections work.
killed and restarted the :5440 compute: row count identical before/after (284), wire-inserted row trip_id=900001 intact — all state lives in Iceberg/RustFS, none in the process.

probes.behavior: multiple stateless computes on shared storage
probes.evidence: second 'icegres serve' on :5440 (same catalog, started before the write) returned identical count (284) and served row trip_id=900001 committed via :5439 after both computes started (visible on first poll, 39ms; each query reloads table metadata from the REST catalog, so no staleness window was observed).
serve spawn -> first successful 'select 1': 33ms (single run, 50ms poll; p50/p95 over >=5 runs is bench cold_start_ms). Neon bar: ~500ms-few s.

snapshots are enumerable (e.g. id 2846737285402141775 via "trips$snapshots") but no snapshot-pinned read exists in datafusion 52 / iceberg-datafusion 0.9: FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF -> 'ERROR: sql parser error: Expected: one of UPDATE or SHARE, found: SYSTEM_TIME'; table@snapshot name -> 'ERROR: Error during planning: table 'icegres.demo.trips@2846737285402141775' not found'
no idle-shutdown supervisor: 'icegres serve --help' has no idle/scale/shutdown option; the process runs until killed. Roadmap item (SPEC §4.5). Cold start of a few seconds (D3) makes this viable.

serve log contains structured fields catalog_uri/warehouse/s3_endpoint/listen_addr: 2026-07-05T17:22:55.375691Z INFO icegres: connecting to Iceberg REST catalog catalog_uri=http://127.0.0.1:8181/catalog warehouse=lakehouse s3_endpoint=http://127.0.0.1:9000 2026-07-05T17:22:55.393333Z INFO icegres: starting pgwire server listen_addr=127.0.0.1:5440
pgwire connect + 'select 1' works as a health probe (this is what the harnesses use). No dedicated HTTP health endpoint exists (curl :5439/health -> 'connection failed').
'icegres serve' booted with ZERO flags — all config from ICEGRES_* env vars (host/port/catalog/warehouse/S3) — and answered on :5444 (demo.cities count=20)
